decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.3°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to improve lateral heat distribution away from the chip hotspot. I will apply a conservative 1.5x increase (from 90.0 to 135.0 W/(m*K)), which is within the allowed [0.5x, 2.0x] step ratio and well below the upper bound of 500.0.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity enhances lateral heat spreading, reducing the local thermal gradient under the chip and lowering the peak temperature.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards or below 85.0°C
More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader layer
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions rather than lateral spreading.
High conductivity materials may be costlier or heavier in physical implementations, though this is a simulation study.
